methodology.name: hegemon-formal-blueprint-dag-v1
methodology.summary: LeanMarathon-inspired formal-security blueprint: every release claim is a reviewed graph node with implementation bindings, call-order constraints, evidence, dependencies, and cheap falsification cases.

nodes.formal_statement: Every cargo-audit advisory or yanked crate that remains in release code or the formal-core checker lockfile is accepted only when the Lean dependency-audit policy preconditions hold: all waivers are well formed and unexpired, and every finding has an exact advisory/package/version/kind waiver match.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Release.DependencyAuditPolicy.accepts_iff_dependency_audit_preconditions` pins the release waiver decision table, companion theorems pin exact-match acceptance and malformed/unwaived rejection precedence, generated vectors are checked by the policy helper, and `scripts/dependency-audit-gate.sh` remains the executable cargo-audit JSON and waiver-file evidence producer. The formal-core checker crate is audited separately so the assurance tooling does not bypass the same dependency discipline.

target_review.notes: The claim is deliberately operational: it proves review coverage for known dependency findings, not absence of future advisories.

falsification_cases.description: A cargo-audit finding with no matching waiver must fail the dependency gate.
The formal-core checker lockfile is audited independently from the root workspace lockfile.
Lean-generated dependency-audit policy vectors must fail on malformed waivers, exact-match drift, and unwaived findings.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node does not claim dependency freshness, advisory database completeness, or upstream advisory quality; it claims theorem-backed waiver accounting for known cargo-audit findings.

nodes.formal_statement: The release PQ-binary audit decision accepts exactly when source, dependency, and binary scans are all clean; any forbidden classical primitive hit in those scan classes rejects, with source findings taking precedence over dependency and binary findings.
nodes.informal_argument: The binary primitive audit is the release-facing check for the design rule that the native node must stay PQ-clean. The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Release.PqBinaryPolicy.accepts_iff_all_scans_clean` pins the release decision table, companion theorems pin rejection precedence, generated vectors are checked by the release audit helper, and `scripts/security-audit.sh` remains the executable scanner for actual source, dependency, and linked-binary evidence.

target_review.notes: The claim is scoped to the audited release node binary and the current forbidden-primitive pattern set.

falsification_cases.description: Forbidden ECC/pairing/RSA/trusted-setup symbols or dependencies must fail the security audit.
Lean-generated release PQ-binary policy vectors must match the release audit policy helper.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks the release audit decision table. It does not prove that grep/strings discover every possible primitive implementation or that every transitive crate is PQ-secure; those remain scanner-pattern and dependency-review obligations.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean transaction-balance transition, a valid balance witness must have concrete balance slots, the native slot delta must equal `fee - value_balance`, the stablecoin/non-native slot rules must hold, a non-stablecoin transaction has zero delta for every non-native asset, and a stablecoin transaction can have a nonzero non-native delta only for the explicitly selected stablecoin asset where the delta equals the authorized issuance amount. Under the explicit AcceptedProofArtifact balance-soundness assumption from proof acceptance to `validBalance`, the same conservation facts lift to accepted proof wrappers.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_has_slots`, `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_rejects_slot_overflow`, `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_native_delta`, `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_stablecoin_rules`, `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_no_stablecoin_non_native_delta_zero`, `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_stablecoin_selected_delta`, `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_stablecoin_non_selected_non_native_delta_zero`, and `Hegemon.Transaction.validBalance_per_asset_transaction_conservation` prove core facts about the executable balance kernel and its no-asset-transmutation boundary as one top-level conservation theorem. `Hegemon.Transaction.AcceptedProofArtifact.accepted_proof_artifact_native_delta`, `Hegemon.Transaction.AcceptedProofArtifact.accepted_proof_artifact_no_stablecoin_non_native_delta_zero`, `Hegemon.Transaction.AcceptedProofArtifact.accepted_proof_artifact_stablecoin_selected_delta`, and `Hegemon.Transaction.AcceptedProofArtifact.accepted_proof_artifact_stablecoin_non_selected_non_native_delta_zero` lift those facts to accepted proof artifacts under the named proof-system balance-soundness assumption. `gen_transaction_vectors` emits Lean-derived native-fee, value-balance, non-native conservation, counterfeit rejection, stablecoin issuance, stablecoin mismatch, native-asset stablecoin rejection, and balance-slot overflow examples. The formal-core gate runs production `transaction-circuit` conformance against `TransactionWitness::balance_slots` and `TransactionWitness::validate`. The existing TLA+ model remains bounded abstraction evidence for the larger transaction balance/nullifier invariant shape.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for TransactionWitness balance-slot and balance-validation behavior, with the TLA+ model retained as bounded abstraction evidence.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the transaction-balance theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production balance-slot or balance-validation behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Removing `BalanceInvariant` or `NullifierUniqueness` from the model must fail the formal inventory check.
When local model checkers are installed, the wrapper can run TLC/Apalache against the transaction model.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks executable balance-slot and validation semantics, and conditionally lifts those facts to accepted proof wrappers under an explicit balance-soundness assumption. It does not prove note commitment hashing, Merkle hash-function security, nullifier derivation, ciphertext hashing, full AIR/proof-system soundness, or complete Rust refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean Merkle-path transition, path folding uses position-bit orientation at every level, accepts the computed root when the sibling depth matches, and rejects mismatched depths or roots.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.foldPath_empty`, `Hegemon.Transaction.foldPath_even_step`, `Hegemon.Transaction.foldPath_odd_step`, `Hegemon.Transaction.verifyPath_accepts_computed_root`, `Hegemon.Transaction.verifyPath_rejects_wrong_length`, and `Hegemon.Transaction.verifyPath_rejects_wrong_root` prove the executable Merkle path control-flow facts. `gen_merkle_vectors` emits Lean-derived empty, left/right orientation, high-position, wrong-root, and wrong-length examples. The formal-core gate checks those vectors against the production `MerklePath::root_with` and `MerklePath::verify_with_depth_and_node` helpers, while `MerklePath::verify` delegates to the same fold helper with the real Poseidon2 `merkle_node` combiner.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for Merkle path fold, position-bit orientation, explicit-depth admission, and root equality semantics. Poseidon2 hash security/equivalence remains outside this claim.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the Merkle path orientation/depth/root theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production Merkle path folding, depth admission, or root comparison away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Changing SmallWood native membership validation away from explicit MERKLE_TREE_DEPTH gating or MerklePath::verify must be caught by transaction-circuit regression coverage.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks Merkle path control flow only. It does not prove Poseidon2 merkle_node cryptographic security, Poseidon2 implementation equivalence, note commitment hashing, nullifier derivation, ciphertext hashing, full AIR/proof-system soundness, or complete Rust refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean note-commitment input grammar, deployed note commitments absorb domain tag 1 over exactly 18 field limbs: value, asset id, recipient key limbs, rho limbs, commitment-randomness limbs, and authorization-public-key limbs, with each 32-byte field split into four 8-byte big-endian limbs. Real asset ids must be canonical Goldilocks-field representatives and must not equal the external padding sentinel, the padding field alias, or the field modulus.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.NoteCommitmentInputs.note_commitment_domain_tag_is_one`, `note_commitment_inputs_have_eighteen_limbs`, `note_commitment_inputs_start_with_value_and_asset`, `note_commitment_inputs_absorb_recipient_rho_randomness_auth`, and the canonical asset-id theorems pin the note-commitment transcript and field-domain boundary. `gen_note_commitment_input_vectors` emits patterned byte-field and asset-id edge cases. The formal-core gate checks those vectors against the shared production `note_commitment_inputs` helper, while focused regressions require P3, SmallWood, and disclosure preimage builders to route through the same helper and require note/public-input/disclosure admission to reject padding-field alias asset ids.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for note-commitment preimage ordering and field encoding, and as production hardening for asset ids that would alias padding or reduce modulo the Goldilocks field.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the note-commitment input or asset-id canonicality theorems must fail the Lean formal gate.

Changing production note-commitment input ordering, 8-byte big-endian field encoding, input count, domain tag, or asset-id canonicality away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
P3, SmallWood, and disclosure commitment-input builders must continue matching the shared transaction-core note_commitment_inputs helper.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo test -p transaction-circuit commitment_inputs_use_shared_core_preimage -- --nocapture && cargo test -p disclosure-circuit disclosure_commitment_inputs_use_shared_core_preimage -- --nocapture

falsification_cases.description: Note, serialized public-input, raw public-input, and disclosure boundaries must reject asset ids that alias padding or exceed the field domain.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo test -p transaction-circuit field_alias_asset_id -- --nocapture && cargo test -p transaction-circuit note_validation_rejects_field_aliasing_asset_ids -- --nocapture && cargo test -p disclosure-circuit reject_padding_field_alias_asset_id -- --nocapture

falsification_cases.description: The release formal-core wrapper must generate note-commitment vectors and run the production conformance and regression tests.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks transcript layout, field-limb encoding, and asset-id field canonicality only. It does not prove Poseidon2 cryptographic security, Poseidon2 implementation equivalence beyond the input transcript, note commitment hiding or binding reductions, nullifier derivation, ciphertext hash derivation, deployed STARK or SmallWood proof-system soundness, or complete Rust/native-node refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean nullifier input grammar, deployed note nullifiers absorb domain tag 2 over exactly six field limbs in one Poseidon2 absorb block: prf_key, note position, and four 8-byte big-endian rho limbs.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.NullifierInputs.nullifier_domain_tag_is_two`, `nullifier_inputs_have_six_limbs`, `nullifier_inputs_start_with_prf_and_position`, `nullifier_inputs_absorb_rho_after_position`, and `nullifier_inputs_one_absorb_block` pin the nullifier transcript shape. `gen_nullifier_input_vectors` emits patterned rho and boundary-position cases. The formal-core gate checks those vectors against the shared production `nullifier_inputs` helper, while focused regressions require P3 and SmallWood nullifier preimage builders to route through the same helper.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for nullifier preimage ordering and field encoding, with production helper reuse across core, Plonky3, and SmallWood paths.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the nullifier input theorems must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production nullifier input ordering, 8-byte big-endian rho encoding, input count, or domain tag away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
P3 and SmallWood nullifier-input builders must continue matching the shared transaction-core nullifier_inputs helper.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate nullifier vectors and run the production conformance and regression tests.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks transcript layout and field-limb encoding only. It does not prove Poseidon2 cryptographic security, Poseidon2 implementation equivalence beyond the input transcript, nullifier PRF pseudorandomness, deployed AIR or SmallWood proof-system soundness, or complete Rust/native-node refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean transaction public-input shape predicate, fixed-width vectors, boolean flags, inactive-field zeroing, active nullifier/commitment nonzero checks, indexed output-slot commitment/ciphertext-hash facts including direct list-index facts, nonempty transaction admission, canonical balance-slot asset ordering, and stablecoin asset membership must hold before verifier-facing public inputs are accepted.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_accepts_valid`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_bad_input_flag`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_inactive_input_nonzero`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_active_input_zero`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_inactive_output_nonzero_commitment`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_empty_transaction`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_bad_slot_zero`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_padding_not_suffix`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_duplicate_asset`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_rejects_stablecoin_missing_asset`, and `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_accepts_stablecoin_present` prove representative public-input shape acceptance and rejection facts over the executable predicate. `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.validPublicInputShape_output_slot_facts_at`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputs.output_slot_at_get_indices`, and helper theorems lift every indexed output slot to active/nonzero-commitment or inactive-zero-commitment-and-ciphertext-hash facts and direct `List.get?` facts for output flags, commitments, and ciphertext hashes. `gen_public_input_vectors` emits Lean-derived malformed flag, inactive nonzero, active zero, empty transaction, balance-slot ordering, and stablecoin-membership examples. The formal-core gate checks those vectors against production `TransactionPublicInputsP3::validate` through the `transaction-circuit` test.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for the TransactionPublicInputsP3 verifier-admission shape. Statement-hash binding and proof-system soundness remain outside this claim.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the public-input shape theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production TransactionPublicInputsP3 shape validation away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate public-input vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks verifier-facing public-input shape admission only. Output-slot facts and direct output-slot indices are public shape facts, not proof of recipient authorization, note plaintext correctness, encryption confidentiality, Poseidon2 implementation equivalence, note commitment hashing, nullifier derivation, ciphertext hash derivation, statement-hash preimage binding, STARK or SmallWood proof-system soundness, or complete Rust refinement. Statement-hash preimage binding is covered separately by formal.transaction-statement-hash-binding.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean transaction public/serialized input binding predicate, the public transaction fields and serialized STARK public-input fields must agree on merkle root, fee, signed value balance, balance-slot assets, and stablecoin binding payload before verifier-facing TransactionPublicInputsP3 construction succeeds; successful binding exposes the exact P3 public-input field facts consumed by the deployed verifier boundary.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputBinding.bindPublicInputs_accepts_valid`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputBinding.validBinding_accepts_stablecoin`, `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputBinding.validBinding_accepts_omitted_balance_assets`, and the companion rejection theorems prove representative binding accept/reject facts over the executable predicate. `stablecoinBindingMatches_true_fields`, `bindPublicInputs_some_implies_p3_binding_facts`, and `validBinding_true_has_p3_binding_facts` lift a successful binding into field-level P3 public-input facts: public merkle root, fee, signed value balance, balance-slot asset selection, input/output flags, and stablecoin payload fields match the serialized inputs used to construct `TransactionPublicInputsP3`. `gen_public_input_binding_vectors` emits Lean-derived valid, omitted-balance-assets fallback, merkle-root mismatch, fee mismatch, signed value-balance mismatch, balance-slot asset mismatch/count mismatch, and stablecoin payload mismatch examples. The formal-core gate checks those vectors against production `transaction_public_inputs_p3_from_parts` through the `transaction-circuit` test.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for the public TransactionPublicInputs / SerializedStarkInputs binding seam and theorem-level P3 public-input field facts. Statement-hash preimage binding is covered separately by formal.transaction-statement-hash-binding; proof-system soundness remains outside this claim.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the public-input binding theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production public/serialized input binding away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate public-input binding vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks public/serialized input field binding before verifier public-input construction, and packages successful binding as P3 public-input field facts. It does not prove Poseidon2 or BLAKE3 implementation equivalence, transaction statement-hash preimage binding, note commitment hashing, nullifier derivation, ciphertext hash derivation, STARK or SmallWood proof-system soundness, or complete Rust refinement. Statement-hash preimage binding is covered separately by formal.transaction-statement-hash-binding.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean transaction proof-wrapper admission predicate, accepted wrappers must pass exact/canonical byte admission, supported backend routing, proof/public-input presence, verifier-facing public-input validity, top-level nullifier/commitment vector agreement with decoded public inputs, wrapper balance-slot agreement, and final verifier acceptance in that order. Accepted wrappers expose those statement-surface facts as a theorem-level conjunction for downstream proof-artifact lifts.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofWrapperAdmission.accepts_iff_proof_wrapper_preconditions` proves the executable predicate, and `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofWrapperAdmission.proofWrapperAccepts_implies_statement_surface` packages accepted exact/canonical byte admission, backend support, proof/public-input presence, verifier-facing public-input validity, nullifier/commitment vector agreement, balance-slot agreement, and verifier acceptance as theorem-level facts. Companion theorems pin valid acceptance, every rejection label, and the codec/backend/public-input/nullifier-vector/commitment-vector/balance/verifier precedence. `gen_proof_wrapper_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against `evaluate_transaction_proof_wrapper_admission`. Production Plonky3 and default SmallWood wrapper verification call the shared admission helper after decoded-field admission and around verifier acceptance, while the tx-proof manifest production summarizer exact-decodes nested proofs, reconstructs verifier public inputs, checks wrapper summary-vector agreement, and routes verifier rejection through the same admission helper.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for proof-wrapper admission ordering, including wrapper nullifier/commitment summary-vector agreement. Scope is admission over decoded predicates and production Plonky3, SmallWood, and manifest verifier result propagation, not proof-system soundness or a proof of bincode internals.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the proof-wrapper admission theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production proof-wrapper admission away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

The tx-proof manifest must reject nested transaction wrappers with trailing bytes, unsupported backend, missing proof bytes, missing serialized public inputs, invalid serialized public inputs, nullifier/commitment vector drift, or balance-slot drift.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate proof-wrapper admission vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks proof-wrapper admission ordering over production-derived decoded predicates, wrapper summary-vector agreement, and verifier results. It does not prove bincode implementation correctness, STARK/SmallWood cryptographic soundness, verifier implementation correctness, statement-hash binding, or complete Rust refinement. Exact/canonical byte consumption remains enforced by production decoders and regressions.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean transaction spend-authorization relation, successful authorization forces the public input-flag vector, public-nullifier vector, and private witness vector to have matching lengths; each indexed public input slot is then either inactive with a zero public nullifier or active and authorized only by a private witness that reconstructs the note commitment, derives the spend-authority public key from the spend secret, derives the public nullifier from the same secret/rho/position tuple, and verifies Merkle membership under the stated root. Accepted proof wrappers imply this relation only under an explicit spend-authorization proof-system soundness assumption. The SmallWood active-auth-link surface additionally requires active input commitment auth limbs to equal PRF-derived spend-auth limbs.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Transaction.SpendAuthorization.authorizeInputSlots_lengths_match` and `transactionSpendAuthorized_witnesses_align_with_public_inputs` prove that successful slot authorization and transaction spend authorization cannot be established over mismatched public flag/nullifier vectors or mismatched private witness vectors. `Hegemon.Transaction.SpendAuthorization.authorizedInputWitness_implies_facts` exposes commitment, spend-authority, nullifier, and Merkle-membership facts for every authorized input witness. `Hegemon.Transaction.SpendAuthorization.authorizeInputSlots_head_active_facts` lifts those facts to the head active public input slot, while `Hegemon.Transaction.SpendAuthorization.authorizeInputSlots_active_input_facts_at` lifts them to any indexed active slot. `Hegemon.Transaction.SpendAuthorization.authorizeInputSlots_input_slot_facts_at` covers every indexed slot, proving inactive slots carry zero nullifiers and active slots expose spend facts. `Hegemon.Transaction.SpendAuthorization.accepted_wrapper_implies_spend_authorization` plus `accepted_wrapper_implies_input_slot_facts_at` tie accepted proof-wrapper verification to total slot authorization through the named `SpendAuthorizationSoundnessAssumption`. Negative theorems reject wrong spend secrets, wrong public nullifiers, and wrong Merkle roots in the executable model. `Hegemon.Transaction.SmallWoodSpendAuthorization.active_auth_link_constraints_imply_pk_auth_eq_derived` and `active_auth_link_wrong_secret_rejects` model the packed SmallWood linear constraints that bind active input note `pk_auth` limbs to the same PRF-derived spend secret used by nullifier derivation.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a non-production spend-authorization relation and explicit soundness-boundary theorem. It does not prove that deployed STARK/AIR or SmallWood verification implies the relation.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the spend-authorization boundary theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
The release formal-core wrapper must include the spend-authorization module in the Lean root and claims ledger.
The packed SmallWood inline-Merkle verifier must reject a witness whose spend secret derives a different active input auth key than the committed note pk_auth.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves the executable spend-authorization relation and its accepted-wrapper assumption boundary, plus the SmallWood active-auth-link constraint surface and production regression. It uses abstract digest arithmetic and does not prove Poseidon2/BLAKE3 implementation equivalence, hash collision resistance, nullifier PRF security, note commitment hiding/binding, complete deployed STARK/AIR or SmallWood proof-system soundness, witness extraction, wallet key custody, note encryption correctness, or complete Rust/native-node refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean accepted-transaction boundary, accepted proof-wrapper verification implies one abstract transaction relation containing statement-surface facts, valid balance slots and per-asset delta facts, public input shape validity, total indexed input-slot authorization facts, and total indexed output-slot facts, but only under explicit balance and spend proof-system soundness assumptions.
nodes.informal_argument: `Hegemon.Transaction.AcceptedTransactionSoundness.accepted_wrapper_implies_transaction_relation` combines accepted proof-wrapper admission with `BalanceSoundnessAssumption` and `SpendAuthorizationSoundnessAssumption`. The relation exposes accepted wrapper statement-surface facts, balance slot validity, public input shape validity, input slot authorization, and output slot validity. Companion theorems lift native and stablecoin per-asset deltas plus head active, arbitrary indexed active, total indexed input-slot facts, and total indexed output-slot facts out of that relation. This is the single Lean theorem boundary that future deployed STARK/AIR or SmallWood soundness work must discharge.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a non-production accepted-transaction soundness boundary. It is deliberately assumption-bearing and does not certify deployed proof-system soundness.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the accepted-transaction soundness boundary theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
The release formal-core wrapper must include the accepted-transaction soundness module in the Lean root and claims ledger.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves the abstract accepted-transaction relation and its assumption boundary only, including public output-slot shape facts. It does not prove deployed STARK/AIR or SmallWood soundness, witness extraction, verifier implementation correctness, cryptographic hash security, proof privacy, note encryption correctness, recipient ownership, plaintext-to-commitment correctness, or complete Rust/native-node refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean canonical transaction verifier boundary, one canonical statement surface containing accepted wrapper admission, public/serialized input binding, theorem-level P3 public-input field facts, statement preimage construction, proof binding-message construction, public nullifier/commitment/ciphertext vector agreement, total input/output-vector binding, indexed input/output-slot statement/binding facts, root/fee/balance-slot/stablecoin core statement binding, value-balance agreement, stablecoin payload agreement, active spend binding to the exact anchor/nullifier statement surface, field agreement, and balance-slot asset agreement is sufficient to derive deployed relation facts, native and per-asset authorized-delta projections, and the existing accepted transaction relation under explicit deployed verifier soundness. The boundary now also states spend-only and balance/public-field-only deployed soundness assumptions, direct split-boundary fact packages, direct input-slot and public-delta consequences from those split assumptions, plus a compatibility theorem back to the monolithic assumption.
nodes.informal_argument: `Hegemon.Transaction.CanonicalVerifierBoundary.deployed_soundness_implies_accepted_transaction_soundness_assumption` collapses the older independent balance and spend assumptions into `DeployedTxVerifierSoundnessAssumption` over `CanonicalTxStatementSurface`. `accepted_wrapper_and_canonical_statement_implies_transaction_relation` then reuses the accepted-transaction theorem, while companion theorems expose public binding validity, P3 public-input binding facts from `canonical_statement_surface_p3_public_input_binding_facts`, statement-preimage length, wrapper preconditions, public shape validity, shape/statement/binding vector agreement for nullifiers, commitments, and ciphertext hashes, input-flag/nullifier vector agreement across public shape, bound inputs, statement preimage, and proof binding message, output-flag/commitment/ciphertext-hash slot facts bound across public shape, statement preimage, and proof binding message, a single `Hegemon.Transaction.CanonicalVerifierBoundary.canonical_statement_surface_core_binding` package for the merkle-root anchor, fee, balance-slot assets/count, value-balance fields, and stablecoin identity/payload fields across public inputs, statement hash, and proof binding message, active and total input-slot facts bound to the exact statement and binding nullifier vectors, native-delta conservation, and indexed input/output-slot facts from the same canonical surface. `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofSystemBoundary.deployed_soundness_canonical_surface_implies_boundary_facts` packages those surface facts with `DeployedTxRelationFacts`, `AcceptedTransactionRelation`, and the core statement/input/output-vector binding into one target for future AIR/refinement work; `Hegemon.Transaction.ProofSystemBoundary.deployed_soundness_canonical_surface_public_input_p3_binding_facts`, `canonical_boundary_facts_core_statement_binding`, `canonical_boundary_facts_native_delta`, `canonical_boundary_facts_authorized_asset_delta`, `canonical_boundary_facts_authorized_asset_delta_value`, `canonical_boundary_facts_input_slot_bound_to_statement`, `canonical_boundary_facts_output_slot_bound_to_statement`, and `deployed_soundness_canonical_surface_exposes_spend_and_balance` expose the exact P3 public-input field package, core binding, native/per-asset authorized-delta facts, total input-slot statement/proof-binding facts, output-slot statement/proof-binding facts, balance-slot, valid-balance, and spend-authorization facts carried by that target. `deployed_soundness_canonical_surface_input_slot_boundary_facts` and `deployed_soundness_canonical_surface_output_slot_boundary_facts` now package those input/output slot projections directly from the canonical surface plus the explicit deployed-verifier soundness assumption for downstream proof/AIR refinement work. `Hegemon.Native.TxLeafCanonicalSurface.native_tx_leaf_deployed_verifier_boundary_facts` then composes the packaged boundary with accepted native tx-leaf/action equality admission, `native_tx_leaf_full_statement_artifact_output_slot_full_binding` packages selected output rows with the assumption-free full statement/artifact facts, and `native_tx_leaf_canonical_artifact_boundary_facts` packages the deployed-verifier boundary together with native artifact equality facts, wrapper surface facts, root/fee/balance-slot/stablecoin identity bindings, spend/balance exposure, authorized asset deltas, and receipt/public-input/proof/backend/ciphertext-payload binding. `native_tx_leaf_canonical_artifact_boundary_active_input_no_theft` exposes active artifact input spend facts with wrapper preconditions, wrapper surface facts, public-input binding, statement preimage, proof binding message, root/anchor binding, statement/proof nullifier-slot alignment, and native receipt/public-input/proof/backend equality gates. The split-soundness extension adds `Hegemon.Transaction.PublicInputBinding.signedMagnitudeMatches_true_eq`, `Hegemon.Transaction.CanonicalVerifierBoundary.canonical_statement_spend_soundness_active_input_bound_to_statement`, `balance_public_field_facts_authorized_asset_delta_value`, `canonical_statement_balance_soundness_public_authorized_asset_delta_value`, and `deployed_soundness_parts_imply_deployed_tx_verifier_soundness_assumption`, making spend authorization and public balance conservation separate future AIR/refinement targets instead of one oversized assumption. nodes.informal_argument: The Lean structure `Hegemon.Privacy.CiphertextPrivacy.CiphertextPrivacyGame` is intentionally not a cryptographic proof. It packages the observer-chain validity predicates from `Hegemon.Privacy.Observer`, equality of public inputs, equality of parsed ciphertext summaries, equal block/action placement, and one explicit proposition `wireIndistinguishable` plus its proof. The theorems `ciphertext_privacy_game_preserves_public_ciphertext_shape`, `ciphertext_privacy_game_preserves_active_output_count`, `ciphertext_privacy_game_preserves_ciphertext_summary_count`, and `ciphertext_privacy_game_summaries_have_chain_format` prove that this boundary preserves all modeled public ciphertext-shape facts before any cryptographic assumption is used. `ciphertext_privacy_game_preserves_public_metadata_leakage` additionally proves that the non-wire public metadata projection is identical for the two worlds; this is the deterministic privacy-game fact needed when raw ciphertext wires differ but are covered by the crypto premise. `ciphertext_privacy_game_boundary_facts` packages those deterministic facts with first-class proof-system zero-knowledge, wallet metadata hygiene, timing/batching policy, and network metadata policy assumptions, and proves full observer, public metadata, and batch/timing projections ignore private witnesses and prover randomness once public wire data is fixed. `Hegemon.Privacy.NativeObserverSurface.native_tx_leaf_active_output_ciphertext_privacy_game_public_metadata_boundary` composes that public-metadata theorem with an active native tx-leaf output slot: both worlds select public observer rows at the same active-output rank, those rows parse to the same public summary, and the same shape/statement/proof-binding commitment and ciphertext-hash indices plus native ciphertext equality gates are carried across the game. `Hegemon.Privacy.NativeObserverSurface.native_tx_leaf_ciphertext_privacy_game_active_output_slot_selects_same_public_summary` packages the same selected-row boundary together with preserved public metadata leakage, preserved batch/timing leakage, and the explicit `wireIndistinguishable` obligation, and `native_tx_leaf_ciphertext_privacy_game_all_active_outputs_select_same_public_boundary` quantifies that package over every active output slot. The new `active_output_public_metadata_boundary_secret_resampling`, `native_tx_leaf_ciphertext_privacy_game_active_output_secret_resampling_boundary`, and `native_tx_leaf_ciphertext_privacy_game_all_active_outputs_secret_resampling_boundary` theorems prove that the selected/all-active native public metadata row package survives independent left/right private-witness and prover-randomness resampling while carrying the explicit proof-system zero-knowledge, wallet metadata hygiene, timing/batching, and network metadata assumptions. `Hegemon.Privacy.NativeObserverSurface.native_tx_leaf_ciphertext_privacy_game_selected_output_wire_da_commitment_boundary` composes that selected-row package with the wallet chain-wire boundary for both worlds: each selected wire is byte-bounded, parses to the same public summary, projects to fixed-length DA bytes, and carries the same public shape, statement, proof-binding, ciphertext-hash, payload-hash, and output-count gates while ML-KEM, AEAD, KDF, RNG, and raw-wire indistinguishability remain explicit assumptions; `native_tx_leaf_ciphertext_privacy_game_all_active_outputs_projected_da_boundary` lifts that projected-DA package to all active output slots. `Hegemon.Privacy.NativeObserverSurface.native_tx_leaf_ciphertext_privacy_game_decrypts_selected_output_to_statement_commitment` composes that selected-row boundary with wallet decrypt success and plaintext-to-commitment binding: the selected public summary is the decrypted ciphertext summary, recipient material authenticates under the explicit crypto predicate, the recovered plaintext commitment equals the public commitment, and the same commitment is indexed in public shape, statement preimage, and proof-binding vectors. `native_tx_leaf_ciphertext_privacy_game_open_assumption_decrypt_da_boundary` packages that selected decrypt/projected-DA evidence with `ciphertext_privacy_game_open_assumption_boundary_facts`, so deterministic observer leakage, secret-resampling stability, same-wire discharge, and proof-system/wallet/timing/network assumptions travel with the native selected-output implementation boundary. `ciphertext_privacy_game_preserves_batch_timing_leakage` proves that the explicit batch/timing projection, including active-output count, public ciphertext count, parsed-summary count, block height, and action index, is also identical under the same valid same-public-input and same-placement premises. `ciphertext_privacy_game_only_open_crypto_obligation` exposes the exact remaining cryptographic premise, and `same_wire_ciphertext_privacy_game_implies_same_allowed_leakage` connects the boundary back to the stronger observer same-leakage theorem when no wire privacy assumption is needed because the public wires are identical. The production regression `encrypt_same_plaintext_to_same_address_uses_fresh_kem_randomness` builds one note and recipient, encrypts it twice through the wallet `RngCore` path with distinct RNG streams, requires different ML-KEM ciphertexts and different chain wire bytes, and confirms both ciphertexts decrypt to the same plaintext. `ciphertext_privacy_game_secret_resampling_boundary_facts` packages the same boundary into an explicit unlinkability-style resampling theorem: after the privacy-game public fields are fixed, independently replacing the left and right private witnesses and prover randomness preserves the public metadata projection and the explicit batch/timing projection, while the raw-wire indistinguishability, proof-system zero-knowledge, wallet metadata, timing/batching, and network metadata assumptions remain first-class fields rather than hidden conclusions. `ciphertext_privacy_game_open_assumption_boundary_facts` closes the review gap by packaging the deterministic public boundary, the secret-resampling boundary, the same-wire full-leakage theorem, and the exact remaining raw-wire, proof-system, wallet, timing, and network assumptions into one theorem-backed boundary object.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a research privacy-game boundary with explicit cryptographic, proof-system, wallet metadata, timing/batching, and network metadata assumptions plus a production wallet randomness regression. The secret-resampling and open-assumption package theorems strengthen deterministic unlinkability coverage, and the native all-active secret-resampling theorem now carries selected-row public metadata packages across independent private-witness/prover-randomness resampling. This node still does not claim to mechanize ML-KEM, AEAD, RNG, or simulator-based zero-knowledge security.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the ciphertext privacy-game boundary theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.

Wallet note encryption must not deterministically identify repeated plaintexts to the same recipient; distinct RNG streams must produce different KEM ciphertexts and chain wire bytes while decrypting to the same plaintext.
The release formal-core wrapper must include the ciphertext privacy-game module and the same-note re-encryption regression.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves a Lean privacy-game boundary around valid observer-chain public shape, public metadata leakage excluding raw ciphertext bytes, explicit batch/timing leakage, same-shape ciphertext wires, independent left/right private-witness and prover-randomness resampling stability for public metadata and batch/timing projections, same-wire discharge to full allowed-leakage equality, selected/all-active native tx-leaf public metadata row-package stability under independent secret/prover-randomness resampling, all-active native tx-leaf public metadata and projected-DA boundaries under the explicit deployed-verifier and ML-KEM/AEAD/KDF/RNG assumptions, selected-row decrypt-to-commitment reconstruction under the explicit wallet crypto-authentication predicate, and a packaged assumption boundary for raw-wire indistinguishability, proof-system zero knowledge, wallet metadata hygiene, timing/batching policy, and network metadata policy; it also production-checks that wallet note encryption consumes fresh KEM randomness for repeated same-plaintext same-recipient encryption. It explicitly leaves `wireIndistinguishable` as the cryptographic premise for raw ciphertext bytes and explicitly treats batch/timing as leakage, not hidden information. It does not prove ML-KEM IND-CCA security, AEAD confidentiality, KDF security, OS RNG quality, simulator-based zero knowledge, proof-system privacy, wallet/network metadata privacy, timing privacy, ciphertext-hash security, or complete wallet/native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean nullifier-state transition, zero nullifiers cannot be staged or imported, successful staging prevents staging the same nullifier again, and successful import prevents both re-importing and re-staging the same nullifier.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Shielded.stage_rejects_zero`, `Hegemon.Shielded.import_rejects_zero`, `Hegemon.Shielded.stage_prevents_duplicate_pending`, `Hegemon.Shielded.import_prevents_reimport`, and `Hegemon.Shielded.import_prevents_restaging` prove the anti-double-spend state behavior over the executable nullifier kernel. `gen_shielded_vectors` emits Lean-derived nullifier examples, and the formal-core gate runs the production `protocol-shielded-pool` conformance test against them. The native node uses that shared helper for mempool staging, block validation, state replay, and author preview.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for protocol-shielded-pool nullifier stage/import behavior.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the nullifier-state theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production nullifier stage/import behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node does not prove full transaction balance conservation, note commitment correctness, or proof-system soundness.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean fork-choice transition, a candidate tip is selected over the current tip exactly when it has greater work, or equal work with greater height, or equal work and height with a lexicographically lower hash.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.higher_work_wins`, `Hegemon.Consensus.lower_work_loses`, `Hegemon.Consensus.equal_work_higher_height_wins`, `Hegemon.Consensus.equal_work_lower_height_loses`, `Hegemon.Consensus.equal_work_height_uses_hash_order`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.same_tip_not_better` prove deterministic two-tip ordering over the executable fork-choice kernel. `gen_consensus_vectors` emits Lean-derived fork-choice examples, and the formal-core gate runs the production `consensus::fork_choice` conformance test against them. `PowConsensus` and native block import both call that helper, and the blueprint now binds native announced-block fork-choice to the side-branch persistence or canonical reorg publication split. The existing TLA+ model remains the bounded aggregate-work/finality abstraction.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for the deterministic fork-choice ordering helper, with the TLA+ finality model retained as bounded abstraction evidence.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the fork-choice ordering theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production fork-choice ordering away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Removing `ForkChoiceInvariant` or `FinalityInvariant` from the model must fail the formal inventory check.
When local model checkers are installed, the wrapper can run TLC/Apalache against the PoW model.
Native announced-block import must bind fork-choice comparison before either canonical reorg publication or noncanonical side-branch persistence can run.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo run --manifest-path scripts/hegemon_formal_core/Cargo.toml -- check-blueprint --claims config/formal-security-claims.json config/formal-security-blueprint.json

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves deterministic two-tip ordering only. It does not prove PoW admission arithmetic, SHA-256 header-hash implementation equivalence, every network partition, timestamp edge case, or long-range adversarial mining schedule.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean PoW admission transition, a block candidate is rejected when checked u64 next-height, pow_bits, timestamp, compact target, hash threshold, claimed cumulative Work48, or Work48 overflow violate the specified arithmetic policy; otherwise the expected cumulative work is accepted.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.compactTarget_rejects_zero_mantissa`, `Hegemon.Consensus.compactTarget_rejects_shifted_zero_target`, `Hegemon.Consensus.compactTarget_rejects_large_exponent`, `Hegemon.Consensus.checkedNextU64_rejects_max`, `Hegemon.Consensus.checkedNextU64_accepts_predecessor`, `Hegemon.Consensus.timestamp_rejects_parent_equal`, `Hegemon.Consensus.timestamp_rejects_median_equal`, `Hegemon.Consensus.timestamp_rejects_future_skew`, `Hegemon.Consensus.checkedWorkAdd_ok`, `Hegemon.Consensus.checkedWorkAdd_rejects_overflow`, `Hegemon.Consensus.powAdmission_rejects_height`, `Hegemon.Consensus.powAdmission_rejects_height_overflow`, `Hegemon.Consensus.powAdmission_rejects_pow_bits`, `Hegemon.Consensus.powAdmission_rejects_insufficient_work`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.powAdmission_accepts_valid` prove core facts about the executable PoW admission table. `gen_pow_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against the production `evaluate_pow_admission` helper used by `PowConsensus` plus the `consensus-light-client` compact-target, hash-threshold, and Work48 cumulative-work helpers used by native import and bridge verification. The blueprint now requires `PowConsensus::apply_block` to propagate `evaluate_pow_admission` before proof verification or node insertion.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for PoW checked next-height, target, timestamp, hash-threshold, and Work48 cumulative-work admission semantics.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the PoW admission theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean header grammar, the signing preimage is the ASCII domain `block` followed by the fixed consensus header fields in the specified little-endian and byte-array order, and is independent of signature, BFT bitmap, and PoW seal payloads; the full header preimage appends signature length/payload, optional bitmap tag/length/payload, and optional PoW seal tag/nonce/pow_bits.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean statement-anchor admission predicate, accepted nonempty block verification must have one statement binding per transaction and every binding anchor must be known in the parent commitment-tree root history before any same-block commitments are applied. target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for parent-history statement-anchor admission. Scope excludes Merkle/hash cryptographic soundness and complete Rust commitment-tree refinement.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean supply-accounting transition, supply digest increases and decreases are checked against the 128-bit bound, underflow and overflow return rejection, no-coinbase native blocks leave supply unchanged, and native coinbase blocks advance supply by the checked subsidy-plus-fee amount.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.increaseSupplyDigest_ok`, `Hegemon.Consensus.increaseSupplyDigest_rejects_overflow`, `Hegemon.Consensus.decreaseSupplyDigest_ok`, `Hegemon.Consensus.decreaseSupplyDigest_rejects_underflow`, `Hegemon.Consensus.nativeSupplyDelta_without_coinbase`, `Hegemon.Consensus.nativeSupplyDelta_with_coinbase`, `Hegemon.Consensus.advanceNativeSupplyDigest_checked`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.advanceNativeSupplyDigest_rejects_supply_overflow` prove the executable accounting kernel. `gen_supply_vectors` emits Lean-derived consensus and native examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against `CoinbaseData::net_native_delta`, `consensus::reward::update_supply_digest`, and native `advance_native_supply_digest`. The native node now rejects supply overflow instead of using saturating addition on import, replay, and mined-block metadata; mining-template construction must fall back to an empty parent-root template when checked supply advancement rejects pending actions. Native block replay must also match `expected_native_supply_from_parts` as `Some(expected_supply)` or return `SupplyDeltaInvalid` before block commitment admission.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for consensus and native supply accounting behavior. Scope is executable accounting only.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the supply-accounting theorems or their proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing consensus coinbase native delta or supply digest update behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Changing native no-coinbase, coinbase fee, or checked supply overflow behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Disconnecting checked native supply advancement from mining-template fallback, or constructing a header from invalid pending-action roots after supply rejection, must fail the blueprint gate.
Native block replay must fail closed with SupplyDeltaInvalid when checked expected-supply reconstruction overflows, before block commitment admission can accept the replay.
Native work preparation must drop pending actions and bind the parent supply digest when pending-action supply advancement would overflow.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean block-subsidy schedule, the monetary constants equal the tokenomics inputs, height zero mints no subsidy, positive heights use a capped halving epoch, the first epoch pays the initial subsidy, the first and second halving boundaries divide that subsidy by powers of two, and the capped shift extinguishes the subsidy permanently.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Consensus.monetaryConstants_match_tokenomics`, `Hegemon.Consensus.blockSubsidy_zero_height`, `Hegemon.Consensus.blockSubsidy_initial_height`, `Hegemon.Consensus.blockSubsidy_first_epoch_last_block`, `Hegemon.Consensus.blockSubsidy_first_halving_boundary`, `Hegemon.Consensus.blockSubsidy_second_halving_boundary`, `Hegemon.Consensus.blockSubsidy_extinct_at_capped_shift`, `Hegemon.Consensus.blockSubsidy_capped_shift_stable_after_extinction`, and `Hegemon.Consensus.blockSubsidy_uses_capped_halving_epoch` pin the executable schedule. `gen_supply_vectors` emits Lean-derived constants and boundary cases, and the formal-core gate checks them against `consensus::reward::block_subsidy`, the compiled consensus constants, and the native supply-vector parser.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean claimed supply-chain replay transition, if an accepted chain prefix returns a final supply digest, then replaying the same ordered supply deltas from genesis returns exactly that final digest; mismatched claimed supply, burn underflow, and supply overflow reject.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Consensus.SupplyInvariant.validated_chain_matches_expected_deltas` proves that accepted claimed supply-chain replay matches the expected delta replay. Companion theorems `valid_step_claims_expected_supply`, `claimed_supply_mismatch_rejects`, and `supply_delta_failure_rejects_step` prove one-step claimed supply binding and rejection behavior. `gen_supply_invariant_vectors` emits Lean-derived valid, counterfeit-claim, underflow, and overflow chain cases, and the formal-core gate checks them against `consensus::reward::validate_supply_chain`. PoW validation now calls the shared expected supply transition helper through a local `pow.rs` wrapper; the blueprint requires the `let Some(expected_supply) = ... else { return InvalidCoinbase }` shape, the immediate comparison to `block.header.supply_digest`, and dominance before PoW admission, proof verification, or node insertion.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for accepted-chain supply replay. Scope is supply-digest replay, not proof-system soundness or privacy.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native coinbase-accounting admission transition, a block is accepted only when it has at most one coinbase action, no-coinbase blocks are accepted without summing fees, and a present coinbase amount equals the checked block subsidy plus checked shielded-transfer fee total.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Native.CoinbaseAccountingAdmission.no_coinbase_accepts`, `Hegemon.Native.CoinbaseAccountingAdmission.multiple_coinbase_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CoinbaseAccountingAdmission.fee_total_overflow_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CoinbaseAccountingAdmission.reward_overflow_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CoinbaseAccountingAdmission.coinbase_amount_missing_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CoinbaseAccountingAdmission.amount_mismatch_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.CoinbaseAccountingAdmission.matching_amount_accepts`, and `Hegemon.Native.CoinbaseAccountingAdmission.multiple_precedes_fee_total_overflow` prove the executable admission table. `gen_coinbase_accounting_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived accept/reject examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against `evaluate_native_coinbase_accounting_admission`, the helper used by native block import and mining validation.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native RPC admission predicates, RPC policy resolution accepts only safe/unsafe/auto/empty tokens with auto and empty resolving to safe for external listeners and unsafe for local listeners; safe policy rejects unsafe methods and hides them from rpc_methods; timestamp queries accept only non-inverted, non-overflowing ranges within the row cap; base64/hex byte strings accept only within raw and decoded caps; and JSON-RPC batches accept only when nonempty and within the batch cap. Blueprint implementation bindings additionally require timestamp, block-row, and wallet archive materialization helpers to propagate storage/key/value failures before returning JSON rows, and require mining thread parameters to pass through a bounded parser before starting mining.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Native.RpcAdmission.rpc_policy_accepts_valid_tokens`, `Hegemon.Native.RpcAdmission.rpc_policy_rejects_invalid`, `Hegemon.Native.RpcAdmission.rpc_policy_auto_external_safe`, `Hegemon.Native.RpcAdmission.rpc_policy_auto_local_unsafe`, `Hegemon.Native.RpcAdmission.safe_policy_rejects_unsafe_methods`, `Hegemon.Native.RpcAdmission.unsafe_policy_allows_every_method`, `Hegemon.Native.RpcAdmission.safe_method_list_hides_unsafe_methods`, `Hegemon.Native.RpcAdmission.timestamp_range_accepts_iff_requested_within_limit`, `Hegemon.Native.RpcAdmission.hex_byte_parse_accepts_iff_caps_hold`, `Hegemon.Native.RpcAdmission.base64_byte_parse_accepts_iff_caps_hold`, and `Hegemon.Native.RpcAdmission.batch_accepts_iff_nonempty_within_limit` prove the executable RPC admission behavior. `gen_rpc_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against production `rpc_method_policy`, `dispatch_rpc_method`, `native_rpc_methods`, `block_timestamps`, `parse_bytes_value`, and `rpc_handler` behavior. Separate blueprint implementation bindings require the production block metadata, timestamp, commitment archive, and ciphertext archive materializers to be called by the exported RPC paths with propagated results, require mining thread parsing before `start_mining`, and focused regressions corrupt sled rows or malformed explicit parameters to prove those paths fail closed.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean legacy network secure-channel predicates, the KDF preimage is the fixed secure-channel-v2 domain followed by one of three distinct i2r/r2i/aad labels and the exact offer, acceptance, confirmation, responder-secret, and initiator-secret byte sequences; initiator and responder roles choose opposite send/receive directional key slots; AES-GCM nonces are four zero bytes followed by the big-endian u64 counter; counter overflow rejects before encrypt/decrypt can advance; and the modeled channel state transition increments only the send counter on successful protect and only the receive counter on successful open, using the matching directional key slot and nonce in each case.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Network.SecureChannel.directional_labels_distinct`, `aad_label_distinct_from_i2r`, `aad_label_distinct_from_r2i`, `send_recv_slots_distinct`, `key_preimage_starts_with_domain`, `sample_i2r_r2i_preimages_distinct`, `nonce_from_counter_zero`, `nonce_from_counter_max`, `nextCounter_accepts_below_max`, and `nextCounter_rejects_u64_max` prove the executable key-schedule and nonce helper behavior. The channel-state theorems `initial_state_counters_zero`, `protectFrame_accepts_below_max`, `openFrame_accepts_below_max`, `protectFrame_rejects_send_overflow`, `openFrame_rejects_recv_overflow`, `protectFrame_direction_and_counter`, and `openFrame_direction_and_counter` lift those helpers into send/receive state transitions with direction-separated slots and monotone per-direction counters. `gen_network_secure_channel_vectors` emits Lean-derived KDF preimage, role, and nonce examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against the production `network` helpers used by `SecureChannel`.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for the legacy network secure-channel key schedule, role mapping, nonce layout, counter-overflow gate, and explicit send/receive channel-state transition theorem. Scope is KDF/nonce/control logic, not primitive cryptographic security or OS RNG quality.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the secure-channel key-schedule and nonce theorems or companion examples must fail the Lean formal gate.

Changing production KDF domain, direction labels, key preimage input ordering, role-to-key mapping, nonce bytes, or counter overflow behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Focused network adversarial tests must keep reconnects from reusing the same first key/nonce pair and must keep opposite directions from sharing the same AES-GCM key at nonce zero.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate secure-channel vectors and run the production conformance test.
Active P2P handshakes must propagate secure-channel construction, and encrypted send/receive must propagate channel encryption/decryption before frame publication or decode.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks legacy network secure-channel KDF preimage labels/order, role-to-directional-key mapping, nonce layout, counter overflow rejection, and pure channel-state send/receive counter transitions. Blueprint bindings additionally require the active P2P handshake and encrypted frame path to propagate secure-channel construction and encrypt/decrypt helpers. It does not prove SHA-256 implementation equivalence, AES-GCM security, ML-KEM/ML-DSA security, OS RNG quality, full PQ Noise handshake correctness, network liveness, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean PQ Noise predicates, the session HKDF salt is the transcript hash, the IKM is shared_secret_1 followed by shared_secret_2, i2r/r2i/aad info labels are distinct for both concrete and role-selected send/receive slots, initiator and responder roles choose opposite send/receive key slots, AES-GCM nonces are four zero bytes followed by the big-endian u64 counter, counter overflow rejects before encrypt/decrypt advances, InitHello/RespHello/Finish signatures hash the exact domain-separated message fields with transcript hashes included only in RespHello and Finish, and the modeled session channel state increments only the send counter on successful protect and only the receive counter on successful open, using the matching directional key slot and nonce in each case. Under explicit transcript, ML-KEM, ML-DSA, HKDF, and AEAD assumptions, accepted authenticated PQ handshakes establish PQ-native direction-separated channel facts and initial PQ Noise protect/open transitions use role-correct slots, AAD separated from directional labels, and zero counters; the legacy secure-channel comparison surface remains separately stated.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Network.PqNoise.hkdf_infos_distinct`, `aad_info_distinct_from_i2r`, `aad_info_distinct_from_r2i`, `aad_info_distinct_from_send`, `aad_info_distinct_from_recv`, `sample_hkdf_ikm_is_ordered_shared_secrets`, `send_recv_slots_distinct`, `nonce_from_counter_zero`, `nonce_from_counter_max`, `nextCounter_accepts_below_max`, `nextCounter_rejects_u64_max`, `init_hello_preimage_starts_with_domain`, `resp_hello_preimage_starts_with_domain`, `finish_preimage_starts_with_domain`, `sample_resp_preimage_binds_transcript_hash`, and `sample_finish_preimage_binds_transcript_hash` prove the executable helper logic. The channel-state theorems `initial_state_counters_zero`, `protectFrame_accepts_below_max`, `openFrame_accepts_below_max`, `protectFrame_rejects_send_overflow`, `openFrame_rejects_recv_overflow`, `protectFrame_direction_and_counter`, and `openFrame_direction_and_counter` lift those helpers into send/receive state transitions with direction-separated slots and monotone per-direction counters. `Hegemon.Network.PqNoiseHandshakeChannel.accepted_authenticated_pq_handshake_establishes_pq_channel_facts` and `accepted_authenticated_pq_handshake_initial_pq_protect_open_facts` compose those facts directly with the PQ Noise channel state that production `NoiseCipher` mirrors, while `accepted_authenticated_pq_handshake_establishes_channel_facts` and `accepted_authenticated_pq_handshake_initial_protect_open_facts` keep the legacy secure-channel comparison theorem. All handshake-to-channel theorems keep transcript binding, ML-KEM agreement, ML-DSA authentication, HKDF, and AEAD soundness explicit. `gen_pq_noise_vectors` emits Lean-derived HKDF-input, role, nonce, and signing-preimage examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against production helpers used by `SessionKeys`, `NoiseCipher`, and `PqHandshake`.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for PQ Noise HKDF labels/input ordering, role mapping, nonce layout, counter-overflow gate, handshake signing preimage grammar, explicit send/receive channel-state transition theorem, PQ-native assumption-explicit handshake-to-channel composition, and legacy secure-channel comparison. Scope is byte/control logic and assumption packaging, not primitive cryptographic security or OS RNG quality.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the PQ Noise HKDF, role, nonce, or signing-preimage theorems or companion examples must fail the Lean formal gate.

Changing production PQ Noise HKDF label text, shared-secret ordering, role-to-key mapping, nonce bytes, counter overflow behavior, or signing preimage field order away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Focused PQ Noise tests must keep ML-KEM response and finish ciphertexts from being reproducible from public transcript-derived seeds.
The release formal-core wrapper must generate PQ Noise vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks PQ Noise session HKDF input labels/order, role-to-directional-key mapping, nonce layout, counter overflow rejection, handshake signing preimage grammar, pure channel-state send/receive counter transitions, a PQ-native assumption-explicit handshake-to-channel composition surface, and the legacy secure-channel comparison surface. It does not prove SHA-256 or HKDF implementation equivalence, AES-GCM security, ML-KEM/ML-DSA security, OS RNG quality, KEM ciphertext correctness, full network liveness, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native sync admission predicates, response ranges are empty when the max-block cap is zero or the requested start is beyond the capped end, otherwise they serve only from the requested start through the minimum of requested end, local best height, and max response window; missing-block requests are empty when max-blocks is zero or the peer is not ahead, otherwise they request from local best plus one through the minimum of peer best and the capped window; inbound response block counts accept iff they are within the configured maximum. Blueprint implementation bindings additionally require admitted sync response ranges to materialize canonical block rows through a propagated helper before any block is pushed into a peer response, and require non-genesis sync rows to decode their native action bodies and verify the decoded action root before that materializer returns.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorems `Hegemon.Native.SyncAdmission.response_range_accepts_iff_from_within_cap`, `Hegemon.Native.SyncAdmission.response_range_empty_when_from_after_cap`, `Hegemon.Native.SyncAdmission.response_range_empty_when_max_blocks_zero`, `Hegemon.Native.SyncAdmission.response_range_some_when_from_within_cap`, `Hegemon.Native.SyncAdmission.missing_request_none_when_announced_not_ahead`, `Hegemon.Native.SyncAdmission.missing_request_none_when_max_blocks_zero`, `Hegemon.Native.SyncAdmission.missing_request_some_when_announced_ahead`, and `Hegemon.Native.SyncAdmission.response_count_accepts_iff_within_limit` prove the executable native sync range and count-admission behavior. `gen_sync_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived normal, capped, empty, zero-cap, saturating-u64, exact-limit, and over-limit examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against production helpers used by `NativeNode::block_range`, `request_missing_blocks`, and inbound `NativeSyncMessage::Response` handling. Separate blueprint implementation bindings require `NativeNode::block_range` to call a propagated canonical block-row materializer after range admission and before response rows are pushed; that materializer must fail closed after decoding non-genesis action bytes and checking the decoded action root through the block-commitment admission helper. Focused regressions corrupt local canonical indexes and canonical action bodies to prove an admitted range errors instead of returning a truncated prefix, mismatched row, or forged body commitment.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native sync response range, missing-block request range, zero-cap fail-closed behavior, saturating u64 edges, and inbound response block-count rejection. Additional implementation bindings and regressions require non-genesis canonical sync rows to decode action bodies and verify decoded action roots before response publication. Scope is semantic sync admission and canonical row materialization after decode, not full block validity or network liveness.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the native sync admission theorems or concrete examples must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production native sync response-range, missing-request, zero-cap, saturating, or response-count behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.
Focused native tests must reject oversized inbound sync responses and keep zero-cap range/request helpers fail-closed.
falsification_cases.gate: cargo test -p hegemon-node native_sync_admission_rejects_oversized_responses --lib --no-default-features -- --nocapture && cargo test -p hegemon-node native_sync_ranges_fail_closed_when_cap_zero --lib --no-default-features -- --nocapture

The release formal-core wrapper must generate native sync admission vectors and run the production conformance test.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks native sync semantic admission after bounded message decode, and production-bindings require non-genesis canonical sync row materialization to decode action bodies and verify decoded action roots before response publication. nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native mineable-action admission predicate, a candidate artifact action is accepted for block-template construction only when it is the selected same-block candidate; a sidecar transfer action is accepted only when its local staged ciphertext metadata is present and size metadata agrees; non-candidate, non-sidecar actions are unaffected by sidecar metadata.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.MineableActionAdmission.accepts_iff_mineable_preconditions` proves the complete executable block-template action admission predicate, with companion theorems for selected candidate acceptance, valid sidecar transfer acceptance, plain action acceptance, unselected candidate rejection, missing sidecar ciphertext metadata, missing sidecar size metadata, sidecar size mismatch, and candidate-before-sidecar precedence. `gen_mineable_action_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived accept/reject examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against the production `evaluate_native_mineable_action_admission` helper used by native candidate selection before `prepare_work` and by mined-block import through the prepared mining-action recovery path. Native regressions show stale or mismatched sidecar transfers plus candidate artifacts produce an empty template, while matching staged sidecar metadata keeps the transfer and selected candidate in work.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native block-template action admission. Scope is the cheap local selection gate before mining work, not recursive proof soundness or candidate-selection fairness.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the native mineable-action admission theorems or concrete examples must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing production native mineable-action admission away from the Lean executable spec must fail the generated-vector conformance step.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native miner-identity transition, a non-genesis native block can be accepted or projected from storage only when its ML-DSA public-key length and parsing are valid, its 48-byte miner commitment matches that public key, its ML-DSA signature length and parsing are valid, and signature verification succeeds over the native signature message; genesis is exempt.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.MinerIdentity.accepts_iff_native_miner_identity_preconditions` proves the executable native miner-identity table, with concrete theorem facts for valid non-genesis admission, genesis-without-identity admission, public-key length and parse failures, miner-commitment mismatch, signature length and parse failures, signature-verification failure, and rejection precedence. `gen_native_miner_identity_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples checked against the production `evaluate_native_miner_identity_admission` helper. The blueprint also requires newly mined native blocks to call `sign_native_block_meta` before PoW metadata verification, atomic commit, state publication, or broadcast; requires storage/projection callers to route through `verify_native_block_meta_projection` before trusting startup canonical metadata, serving canonical sync rows, or exporting bridge witness light-client material; requires `verify_native_pow_meta` to propagate `verify_native_miner_identity` before light-client PoW verification; and requires the signing and verification paths to share `native_miner_signature_message`, whose Rust regressions bind nonce and work hash.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native self-contained ML-DSA miner side-auth admission. Miner registry governance and ML-DSA security remain outside this node.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening native miner-identity theorems or proofs must fail the Lean formal gate.

It does not prove ML-DSA unforgeability or implementation equivalence, miner registration or stake governance, OS RNG quality for local identity generation, migration of legacy unsigned non-genesis dev-chain metadata, sled durability, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean mined-work admission transition, native local mined-block import accepts work as fresh only when the current best hash equals the work parent hash and the current best height has a checked u64 next height equal to the work height; rejection precedence is fixed by the executable table.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.MinedWorkAdmission.accepts_iff_mined_work_preconditions` proves the complete executable mined-work freshness predicate, with concrete theorem facts for valid admission, parent-hash mismatch, height mismatch, u64 height overflow rejection, parent-before-height rejection precedence, and accepting height u64::MAX only when the parent is u64::MAX - 1. `gen_mined_work_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples checked against the production `evaluate_native_mined_work_admission` helper called by `import_mined_block` before pending-action replay, body commitment admission, supply accounting, and PoW metadata verification. The blueprint also gates local mined-block write order: `verify_native_pow_meta` must run before the atomic mined-block sled commit, in-memory state publication, or broadcast; `commit_mined_block_atomically` must commit the block record, height index, best pointer, canonical state/replay/ciphertext indexes, and pending-action removals before `publish_mined_state` or broadcast; and regressions assert invalid local seals leave pending actions, commitment state, and supply untouched while a mined action block reloads from canonical sled state after reopening.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native local mined-work freshness admission. Scope excludes work-template construction, pending-action replay, body commitment replay, supply accounting, and PoW threshold verification.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the mined-work admission theorem or concrete overflow/rejection facts must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing the production mined-work freshness admission table away from the Lean executable spec must fail generated-vector conformance.
Native local mined-work import must reject a best tip at u64::MAX instead of saturating and accepting same-height work.
Native local mined-block import must reject an invalid mined seal before mutating pending actions, commitment state, or supply state.

The blueprint gate must reject the mined-work admission claim if local mined-block PoW metadata verification drifts after the atomic mined-block sled commit, in-memory state publication, or block broadcast.
