nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks native local mined-work freshness admission, and the blueprint/regression gate enforces that final local PoW metadata verification precedes the atomic mined-block sled commit, in-memory state publication, and broadcast, and that the atomic commit precedes in-memory state publication plus broadcast. Mined import now commits the block record, height index, best pointer, canonical state/replay/ciphertext indexes, and pending-action removals in one sled transaction before publishing the next in-memory state. Nonwinning announced side-branch block-record reload is covered separately by the native block replay-refinement implementation-binding gate. It does not prove mining work template construction, pending-action replay, body commitment replay, action payload validation, supply accounting, PoW hash-threshold validity beyond the production verifier call, wall-clock correctness, fork-choice, sled transaction implementation correctness, filesystem/fsync durability, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean storage-durability admission transition, critical native storage mutations may be treated as publishable or complete only when the storage transaction/write step accepted and the explicit durability flush step accepted; transaction rejection precedes flush-failure reporting.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.StorageDurabilityAdmission.accepts_iff_storage_durability_preconditions` proves the complete executable predicate, and concrete theorems pin valid acceptance, durability-flush rejection, and transaction-before-flush rejection precedence. `gen_storage_durability_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived mined-block, canonical-reorg, canonical-index-repair, noncanonical-record, pending-action, ciphertext-sidecar, proof-sidecar, genesis-bootstrap, genesis-marker-repair, startup staged-ciphertext repair, startup staged-proof repair, startup pending-action repair, transaction-rejected, and flush-failed examples checked against the production `evaluate_native_storage_durability_admission` helper. The production `flush_native_db_durability_barrier` performs a database flush and maps failures through the same rejection table; `flush_native_durability_barrier` routes initialized-node paths to that helper. Blueprint bindings require mined-block import to propagate the barrier before prepared-work cleanup, in-memory publication, or broadcast; canonical reorg to propagate it before publication; canonical-index repair and nonwinning side-branch persistence to propagate it before completion; pending-action and sidecar staging to propagate it before in-memory publication; genesis bootstrap/marker repair to propagate it before startup succeeds; and startup staged-sidecar and pending-action quarantine cleanup to propagate the DB durability barrier before the repaired startup state can be returned for publication.

target_review.notes: Accepted as Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for the explicit native storage durability barrier. Scope is the admission table and production wiring around sled transaction/write success plus explicit database flush; it does not prove sled or operating-system crash semantics.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the storage-durability admission theorems or concrete precedence facts must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing the production storage-durability admission table away from the Lean executable spec must fail generated-vector conformance.

The blueprint gate must reject storage durability if mined-block import, canonical reorg, canonical-index repair, noncanonical block-record persistence, pending-action staging, sidecar staging, startup staged-sidecar repair, startup pending-action repair, or genesis bootstrap/repair stop propagating the explicit durability barrier or move publication before it.

nodes.scope_boundary: This node proves and vector-checks the explicit storage durability admission table and binds production critical mutation paths to propagate the database flush barrier before publication or success, including startup genesis bootstrap/marker repair, startup staged-sidecar repair, startup pending-action quarantine cleanup, and user-facing pending-action/sidecar staging. It reduces the filesystem durability residual by making the barrier explicit and gated, but it does not prove sled transaction implementation correctness, operating-system fsync semantics, disk firmware honesty, crash-consistency below sled, or complete native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean atomic-commit manifest transition, native mined-block commits, canonical reorg commits, canonical-index repairs, and noncanonical block-record persistence accept only when their declared row-family write, clear, and removal counts match the kind-specific source counts before storage mutation. Canonical reorg acceptance additionally requires the replacement chain and its block-record/height-index write-set projection to pass the Lean-modeled structural admission table before canonical replay, index planning, or commit.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.AtomicCommitManifestAdmission.accepts_iff_atomic_commit_manifest_preconditions` proves the executable manifest predicate, with concrete valid-kind and mismatch rejection theorems for mined plan length, block-record writes, height-index writes, best-pointer writes, canonical-index clears, pending-tree clears, pending removals/writes, commitment writes, nullifier writes, bridge replay writes, ciphertext index/archive writes, and staged ciphertext removals. The companion theorem `Hegemon.Native.CanonicalReorgChainAdmission.accepts_iff_canonical_reorg_chain_preconditions` proves the canonical reorg replacement-chain gate over structural chain predicates, best-tip agreement, and block-record/height-index projection equality. `gen_atomic_commit_manifest_admission_vectors` and `gen_canonical_reorg_chain_admission_vectors` emit Lean-derived cases checked against production helpers. The native commit helpers build manifests from the same action/planning/index data they use for persistence and must propagate the gates before noncanonical record persistence, canonical reorg replay/planning, or any atomic sled transaction.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a bounded implementation-equivalence hardening slice for native atomic commit manifests and canonical reorg chain/write-set projection. It proves the declared row-family manifest gate, the replacement-chain admission table, and production ordering, not sled internals or OS durability.

falsification_cases.description: Removing or weakening the atomic commit manifest theorem or its concrete mismatch theorems must fail the Lean formal gate.
Changing native atomic commit manifest admission behavior away from the Lean executable spec must fail generated-vector conformance.
Changing canonical reorg replacement-chain or write-set projection admission away from the Lean executable spec must fail generated-vector conformance.

The blueprint gate must reject native commit paths that stop calling or propagating the manifest gate before noncanonical block-record persistence or sled transaction execution, or canonical reorg paths that stop admitting the replacement chain before replay/planning/commit/publication.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean work-template admission transition, native mining template construction emits a work height only when the current best height has a checked u64 successor and the cumulative Work48 value can be advanced for the configured PoW bits; rejection precedence is fixed by the executable table.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.WorkTemplateAdmission.accepts_iff_work_template_preconditions` proves the complete executable template-admission predicate, with concrete theorem facts for valid admission, u64 height overflow rejection, cumulative-work overflow rejection, height-before-work rejection precedence, and accepting height u64::MAX only when the parent is u64::MAX - 1. `gen_work_template_admission_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples checked against the production `evaluate_native_work_template_admission` helper called by `prepare_work` before header preimage construction. Direct regressions cover the former `saturating_add(1)` height edge and the former `cumulative_work_after(...).unwrap_or(best.cumulative_work)` fallback.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native mining work-template admission. Scope excludes pending-action preview, body replay, supply accounting, PoW seal liveness, and full native-node equivalence.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native sidecar upload admission predicates, ciphertext/proof upload requests accept only within request-count caps, staged sidecar insertions accept only when capacity remains or the item replaces an existing staged entry, proof metadata accepts only when binding_hash is present and valid and proof is present, and decoded proof bytes accept only when nonempty, within the native tx-leaf artifact cap, and whose decoded proof binding hash equals the upload key.

nodes.formal_statement: For the Lean pre-heavy-work resource surface, if the resource-budget, RPC admission, sidecar upload, mineable-action, action-request projection, transfer-payload, candidate-artifact, tx-leaf artifact parser, and receipt-root schedule surfaces are all accepted, then the combined surface exposes the concrete resource facts needed before expensive work: pending-action and staged-proof byte totals are within caps, timestamp rows and RPC batches are within caps, hex/base64 decoded-byte inputs satisfy raw and decoded caps, submit-action JSON/kernel-envelope/route/nullifier/public-args/exact-payload projection predicates hold, sidecar upload request/capacity/proof predicates satisfy their preconditions, mineable candidate/sidecar action predicates satisfy their preconditions, transfer proofs and inline ciphertexts are in cap, recursive candidate proofs and tx counts are in cap, tx-leaf artifact parser shape facts hold, and receipt-root expected leaves, leaf/fold counts, and fold-shape schedule facts hold.
nodes.informal_argument: The theorem `Hegemon.Native.PreHeavyWorkResourceBoundSurface.accepted_preheavy_resource_bound_surface_exposes_bounds` composes accepted facts from `Hegemon.Native.ResourceBudgetAdmission`, `Hegemon.Native.RpcAdmission`, `Hegemon.Native.SidecarUploadAdmission`, and `Hegemon.Native.MineableActionAdmission`. The theorem `Hegemon.Native.PreHeavyWorkResourceBoundSurface.action_request_projection_accepts_implies_preheavy_bounds` derives the strict submit-action projection preconditions from accepted action-request projection, including JSON decode acceptance, absent kernel-envelope fields, supported route, scoped/bounded/hex-valid nullifiers, bounded/base64-decoded public args, and exact route-payload decoding. The theorem `Hegemon.Native.ReceiptRoot.receipt_root_schedule_accepts_implies_facts` exposes nonzero expected leaves, exact leaf/fold-count agreement, and exact fold-shape schedule acceptance from the receipt-root structural gate. The strengthened theorem `accepted_preheavy_public_input_parser_admission_bounds_verification_paths` adds those action-request bounds, accepted transfer-payload bounds, candidate-artifact bounds, tx-leaf artifact byte-shape facts, and receipt-root schedule facts while carrying parser correctness and benchmark caps as explicit assumptions. It derives the resource/capacity/precondition facts using the existing iff and precondition theorems, so it adds a single review target for the pre-heavy-work DoS boundary without changing production runtime behavior. Production conformance remains inherited from the generated-vector gates for the dependency surfaces.

target_review.notes: Accepted as a Lean composition over existing theorem-backed admission surfaces, now including strict action-request projection facts, transfer payload, candidate artifact, tx-leaf artifact parser bounds, and receipt-root schedule facts. nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native pending-action reload predicate, a persisted pending action is accepted into the reloaded local mempool only when its key is well formed, its embedded tx_hash equals the 32-byte sled key, the production pending_action_hash recomputation equals the embedded tx_hash, and no equal action hash has already been loaded; otherwise startup reload rejects with fixed malformed-key, key/hash-mismatch, recomputed-hash-mismatch, then duplicate precedence. Accepted reload can now be carried into the byte parser and publication refinement packages together with exact decode, block-action declared-count binding, action-hash, wire replay, and canonical publication facts.
nodes.informal_argument: The Lean theorem `Hegemon.Native.PendingActionReload.accepts_iff_pending_action_reload_preconditions` proves the executable key/hash acceptance predicate, while `Hegemon.Native.PendingActionReload.malformed_action_key_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.PendingActionReload.key_hash_mismatch_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.PendingActionReload.recomputed_hash_mismatch_rejects`, `Hegemon.Native.PendingActionReload.duplicate_pending_action_rejects`, and the companion precedence theorems pin the reject order. `gen_pending_action_reload_vectors` emits Lean-derived examples, and the formal-core gate checks them against `evaluate_native_pending_action_reload`, the helper used by `load_pending_actions`. Blueprint bindings and focused regressions additionally require persisted local mempool entries to be semantically unique after removing arrival time from their hash preimage, require semantically re-admitting those loaded entries before they can feed mining selection, and require canonical reorg pending resurrection to re-admit existing pending actions before orphaned old-chain actions can be persisted. `Hegemon.Native.PendingActionByteParserRefinement.accepted_pending_action_byte_parser_refines_wire_replay_rows` derives projected replay row-count equality from exact pending/block-action decode, accepted pending reload, accepted wire replay, and the production count binding `wireProjection.actionCount = blockActionDecode.declaredTxCount`. `Hegemon.Native.PendingActionBytePublicationRefinement.accepted_pending_action_bytes_bind_tx_leaf_publication` then carries that accepted parser/reload surface through action-hash admission, raw canonical publication, and native tx-leaf/canonical statement artifact facts.

target_review.notes: Accepted as real Lean theorem evidence plus generated Rust conformance vectors for native pending-action startup key/hash reload. Semantic startup re-admission and canonical reorg pending resurrection are covered by Rust implementation bindings and focused regressions; sled atomicity, filesystem durability, SCALE decode equivalence, and pending_action_hash cryptographic security remain outside Lean.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native block-artifact binding transition, accepted tx-leaf/action pairs must agree on nullifiers, commitments, ciphertext hashes, input/output flag counts, version, fee, stablecoin public payload, balance-tag projection, receipt statement hash, public-input digest, proof digest/backend profile, and ciphertext payload hashes; accepted candidate artifacts must agree with the block's computed DA root, tx statement commitment, and verified recursive state root.

nodes.formal_statement: For the executable Lean native tx-leaf canonical-surface composition, an accepted native tx-leaf/action binding gate plus a canonical deployed transaction statement surface assumption-free expose statement preimage success and length, proof binding-message success, public-input binding validity, proof-wrapper preconditions and statement surface facts, public-shape validity, core root/fee/value-balance/balance-slot/stablecoin statement binding, full nullifier/commitment/ciphertext vector binding, input/output vector binding, value-balance binding, stablecoin payload binding, tx-leaf/action preconditions, full tx-leaf/action equality facts, and native receipt/public-input/proof/backend/ciphertext-payload equality gates. A selected output-slot full-binding theorem packages that statement/artifact surface with output-slot facts, statement/proof-binding output rows, wrapper/public/statement facts, native commitment/ciphertext/output-count equality gates, and native artifact digest/backend gates. With the explicit deployed verifier soundness assumption, the same boundary implies the accepted transaction relation, field-level tx-leaf/action binding facts, authorized per-asset delta values for the modeled balance witness, active and inactive input-slot authorization facts bound to statement/proof-binding vectors, packaged native artifact input-slot facts, active input no-theft facts with wrapper/statement/proof/native equality gates, and output commitment/ciphertext-slot facts bound to those same vectors. The proof-keyed transfer payload package additionally composes accepted payload admission with the canonical native tx-leaf artifact boundary, so active input no-theft facts carry payload binding-hash agreement, decoded proof binding-hash agreement, payload fee agreement, public-input binding, root/anchor binding, statement/proof nullifier-slot alignment, and native fee/proof/backend/ciphertext-payload equality gates in one full-binding theorem; every proof-keyed input slot also carries the total authorization-or-inactive-zero full-binding package with the same payload, wrapper, root, statement/proof nullifier-vector, and native equality gates. The validated transfer boundary further binds accepted decoded transfer payload admission and singleton block-action validation acceptance, including payload-before-order and order-before-state precedence facts, to both the active-input no-theft full-binding package and the total input-slot authorization-or-inactive-zero full-binding package before modeled transfer state mutation, and spend-only variants prove those same transfer packages from `DeployedTxVerifierSpendSoundnessAssumption` without requiring balance/public-field soundness.
nodes.informal_argument: `Hegemon.Native.TxLeafCanonicalSurface.tx_leaf_action_accepts_implies_binding_facts` extracts nullifier, commitment, ciphertext-hash, active-count, version, fee, stablecoin payload, balance-tag, receipt statement-hash, public-input digest, proof digest/backend, and ciphertext payload-hash equality facts from the accepted native tx-leaf/action gate. `native_tx_leaf_binding_and_canonical_surface_statement_proof_facts` composes those equality facts with the canonical statement surface without the deployed-verifier soundness assumption, exposing statement preimage success/length, proof binding-message success, public-input binding validity, proof-wrapper preconditions, wrapper statement-surface facts, and the native receipt/public-input/proof/backend/ciphertext-payload equality gates. `native_tx_leaf_binding_and_canonical_surface_full_statement_artifact_facts` strengthens that assumption-free package by also exposing public-shape validity, the reusable core statement binding, full statement/proof vector binding, input/output vector binding, value-balance binding, stablecoin payload binding, tx-leaf/action preconditions, and the full `TxLeafActionBindingFacts` record, so future AIR/Rust-refinement proofs cannot omit version, fee, active counts, balance tag, stablecoin payload, vector equality, or artifact digest/backend fields from the target surface before invoking verifier soundness. `native_tx_leaf_full_statement_artifact_output_slot_full_binding` then specializes that package to selected output rows, carrying output-slot facts, statement/proof-binding row facts, wrapper/public/statement facts, output commitment/ciphertext/output-count native gates, and artifact digest/backend gates together for future output AIR, ciphertext, and observer-refinement work. `native_tx_leaf_binding_and_canonical_surface_implies_transaction_relation` composes the same boundary with `CanonicalVerifierBoundary.accepted_wrapper_and_canonical_statement_implies_transaction_relation`; the asset-delta companion theorems then reuse `AssetIsolation.accepted_transaction_relation_authorized_asset_delta` and `accepted_transaction_relation_authorized_asset_delta_value`. The input/output-slot companion theorems reuse the canonical verifier boundary's statement-vector binding theorems so the native artifact boundary reaches active input spend facts, total input-slot facts, and output commitment/ciphertext facts under the same explicit deployed-verifier assumption. `native_tx_leaf_deployed_boundary_input_slot_facts` and `native_tx_leaf_canonical_artifact_boundary_input_slot_facts` expose total input-slot statement/proof-binding facts directly from the packaged deployed-verifier and native artifact boundaries. The traced Lean transition has the same result as the untraced transition, and accepted replay has the canonical stage trace action-stream, supply, block-commitment, accepted. In particular, an accepted multi-action replay summary cannot carry a counterfeit state-root predicate, claimed supply, stale leaf cursor, stale spent-nullifier state, or stale consumed-bridge-replay state under the modeled production-derived predicates. preconditions. `replayInputFromRawDecodedBlock_projects_decoded_fields`, `raw_decoded_blocks_projection_round_trips`, `valid_raw_projected_ledger_state_after_accepts`,
